# Research: NTTR Locations for Surface Attack Training

This document maps the four phases of the Surface Attack Course to specific locations on the **DCS: Nevada Test and Training Range (NTTR)** map. Each location is selected for its terrain suitability, proximity to airfields, and existing range infrastructure.

## Phase 1 Location: Range 63B (The Container Village)

*   **Location Name:** Range 63B / "The Containers"
*   **Coordinates:** N 36°58'00" W 116°02'30" (Approx) | MGRS: 11S NV 86 92
*   **Elevation:** ~4,200 ft MSL
*   **Nearest Airfield:** Creech AFB (KINS) - ~25 NM Southeast
*   **Access:**
    *   **From Creech:** Depart RWY 31/13, turn North-West. Follow US-95 North.
    *   **TACAN:** BTY (Beatty) 140 Radial for 15 NM.
    *   **Visual:** Look for the large distinct grid of shipping containers in the open desert valley north of the Spotted Range.
*   **Suitability:**
    *   **Terrain:** Flat, open desert floor surrounded by low hills. Perfect for safe 4G pull-outs and pattern work.
    *   **Visibility:** High contrast targets (containers) make visual acquisition easy for basic students.
    *   **Pattern:** Sufficient airspace for a standard box pattern (left or right) without terrain conflict.
*   **Required Scenery:**
    *   Place a group of *unarmed* truck targets (e.g., Ural-375) near the container stacks.
    *   Add Red Smoke at the run-in point (IP) 3 NM south of the target to aid alignment.

## Phase 2 Location: Range 64 (Dreamland / Groom Lake Approaches)

*   **Location Name:** Range 64A / East of Groom Lake
*   **Coordinates:** N 37°18'00" W 115°40'00" (Approx) | MGRS: 11S PV 25 35
*   **Elevation:** ~4,500 ft MSL
*   **Nearest Airfield:** Groom Lake (KXTA) - ~10 NM West (Restricted); Lincoln County (KLCN) - ~20 NM North-East.
*   **Access:**
    *   **From Creech:** Long transit (~60 NM). Recommended departure from Groom Lake if accessible, or air-start.
    *   **TACAN:** GFK (Groom Lake) 090 Radial for 12 NM.
    *   **Visual:** The dry lake beds east of the main Groom Lake facility.
*   **Suitability:**
    *   **Hard Targets:** Existing bunker complexes and airfield structures nearby for TGP point-track practice.
    *   **Sensor Work:** Flat background provides good contrast for IR/TV sensors (Shkval/TGP).
    *   **Space:** Massive airspace for high-altitude LGB drops (Angels 15+) without traffic conflict.
*   **Required Scenery:**
    *   Place static *Bunker* objects and *T-72* tanks (active but weapons hold) to practice sensor lock and Maverick hand-off.
    *   Ensure targets are spaced out to allow individual designation.

## Phase 3 Location: Goldfield (The Town)

*   **Location Name:** Goldfield
*   **Coordinates:** N 37°42'30" W 117°14'00" (Approx) | MGRS: 11S MV 79 73
*   **Elevation:** ~5,700 ft MSL
*   **Nearest Airfield:** Tonopah Test Range (KTNX) - ~20 NM South-East.
*   **Access:**
    *   **From Tonopah:** Depart RWY 32, turn North-West following US-95.
    *   **TACAN:** TPH (Tonopah) 310 Radial for 22 NM.
    *   **Visual:** A distinct small town grid with a highway running through it, located in a valley.
*   **Suitability:**
    *   **Urban Clutter:** Essential for CAS "Talk-On" training. Buildings obscure line-of-sight, forcing pilots to use precise 9-line descriptions.
    *   **Collateral Damage:** Simulate "Civilians" (neutral units) to enforce strict ROE.
    *   **Keyhole CAS:** Surrounding hills provide natural "Hold" points for Keyhole anchors (North/South).
*   **Required Scenery:**
    *   Place *Insurgent* infantry and *light vehicles* (UAZ/Technicals) in the streets.
    *   Place a *JTAC* unit (Humvee with radio) on a hill overlooking the town (e.g., "Observation Point Alpha").
    *   Place *Blue Force* friendlies clearly marked (e.g., blue smoke) outside the town to practice danger close.

## Phase 4 Location: Tolicha Peak (Electronic Combat Range)

*   **Location Name:** Tolicha Peak / ECR
*   **Coordinates:** N 37°17'00" W 116°47'00" (Approx) | MGRS: 11S NV 22 28
*   **Elevation:** ~4,000 - 6,000 ft MSL (Varied terrain)
*   **Nearest Airfield:** Tonopah Test Range (KTNX) - ~30 NM North.
*   **Access:**
    *   **From Tonopah:** Depart South-East.
    *   **TACAN:** BTY (Beatty) 340 Radial for 18 NM.
    *   **Visual:** Distinctive peaks and valleys with radar domes (ECR emitters) visible on summits.
*   **Suitability:**
    *   **Complex Terrain:** Requires the FAC-A to manage altitude deconfliction (Wedding Cake) carefully due to terrain masking.
    *   **Threat Emitters:** Existing EWR/SAM radars (simulated or real) add realism for the FAC-A to manage SEAD stacks.
    *   **Orbit:** Good valley for the "Working" stack (Angels 10-14) while high cover holds above.
*   **Required Scenery:**
    *   Place a *SAM site* (SA-6 or SA-8) in "Weapons Hold" status to act as a threat that must be suppressed.
    *   Spawn multiple AI flights (A-10s or F-16s) to orbit at "High Cover" for the student FAC-A to manage.
